opp.name: Aspen S2C
opp.next_step: Pricing quote to be shared , while sending across some business use case .
opp.ais_why:
• Apr 2026 Avoma call captures Caroline Lacocque pitching the 'AI-forward platform' with autonomous agents and native AI to Aspen Holdings. Aspen's buyer Roeland engaged actively on the call but his questions centred on operational topics ‚Äî data migration, support model, SAP integration, pharma reference customers ‚Äî and did not lead with AI-specific asks. AI was Zycus-positioned; buyer engagement was operational.
• Modest AI products in scope (Merlin Intake and ML iAnalyze) alongside traditional S2C modules (iContract, iSave, iSource, iSupplier). BD_Engagement is Engaged. Recent LastActivityDate 2026-05-08. A business case has been requested by Aspen ‚Äî the deal is moving forward but the buyer's stated focus is operational fit not AI capability.
• Medium confidence: clear call evidence of mixed signals ‚Äî Zycus pitched AI but Aspen engaged on operational fundamentals. The customer is engaged but not AI-driven. Defaulted to 5 at the 5/6 boundary as AI is not the explicit selection criterion. Would move UP to 7 if Roeland or a colleague returns with AI-specific follow-up questions; would move DOWN to 4 if the SAP integration / pharma references dominate evaluation.

sources: Avoma discovery call 22 Apr 2026, Roeland Mvokwe: unified platform need, CLM tool dissatisfaction
Avoma note: 'Aspen requires a unified platform covering strategic sourcing and contract lifecycle management to replace disparate tools'
Zycus reference case (Regeneron pharma): 70% cycle-time improvement, 200% onboarding speed, 20% performance gain (Caroline, call 22 Apr)
Roeland's explicit ask for business case with Aspen metrics to validate ROI
metrics.narrative: Roeland Mvokwe articulated operational pain around needing a unified strategic sourcing and contract lifecycle management platform to replace disparate tools, stating 'we want we would love to have a one platform having all of all of the capabilities that we wanted from the street sourcing' (discovery call 22 Apr 2026). He noted their existing CLM tool 'we do not see a value from the strategic sourcing point of view from that CLM tool', signaling dissatisfaction with current state. However, no quantified business impact was captured (no cost, headcount, cycle-time savings, error-rate reduction, or ROI target stated). Zycus reference case cited 70% cycle-time improvement, 200% faster supplier onboarding, 20% supplier performance gain, but Aspen's own baseline metrics were never established. To complete Metrics coverage, Zycus must co-develop a quantified value case with Roeland using Aspen's current-state data (the exact ask Roeland made: 'work on a business case for us... give us an idea of what the cost model or the pricing model... and we're just gonna look at an example').

sources: Avoma 22 Apr, Roeland: proactive outreach at awards dinner, articulated pain, requested business case, laid out approval path
Roeland's detailed questioning on call (SAP integration, implementation, support, pharma references) signals genuine interest and internal advocacy intent
No follow-up engagement post-call: no reply to promised deliverables (business case, pharma references, pricing), no logged Tasks, no joint calls with CIO/manager/Kelly-Ann
49 days since LastActivityDate, 65 days since discovery call — champion cooling risk
champion.narrative: Roeland Mvokwe is the developing champion. He initiated contact with Zycus proactively (reached out at an awards dinner, then agreed to the discovery call 22 Apr 2026), articulated Aspen's pain and unified-platform need, requested pharma references and a tailored business case, and laid out the internal approval path ('bring Zycus to speak to both myself, manager, as well as the CIO'). His engagement on the call was active and detailed (asked about SAP integration, implementation speed, support, supplier onboarding, data migration, pharma proof points), and he explicitly committed to driving the opportunity forward internally ('I can move forward with business case and be able to go into an RFP later on'). However, he has not yet demonstrated access to or influence over the Economic Buyer (CIO) or Decision Maker (Kelly-Ann Schooling) — no introductions made, no joint calls scheduled. His advocacy is potential, not proven: he is building the internal case but has not yet mobilized leadership on Zycus's behalf. Since the call (65 days ago), there has been no follow-up engagement (no reply to Zycus's promised deliverables, no Tasks logged), raising the risk that Roeland is stalled internally or that competing priorities have taken over. Champion strength is developing-to-weak and at risk of cooling further without re-engagement.

sources: Salesforce Competitors__c: Coupa
Avoma 22 Apr, Caroline: 'I know that you're actually currently using another system... the difference is actually that we much more AI forward' (referring to Coupa)
Avoma note: 'Aspen currently uses Coupa and is exploring Zycus as an alternative, noting Coupa's marketing focus versus Zycus's tech development emphasis'
Avoma 22 Apr, Caroline: Zycus differentiation via private ownership, single-source code, native AI, R&D focus vs Coupa's bolt-on Navi AI and change-order model
Roeland's critique: existing CLM tool (possibly Coupa CLM) inadequate for strategic sourcing
competition.narrative: Coupa is the incumbent and the only named competitor. Roeland stated on 22 Apr that 'you're actually currently using another system' (confirmed as Coupa in Salesforce Competitor field and Avoma notes: 'Aspen currently uses Coupa and is exploring Zycus as an alternative'). Caroline positioned Zycus as differentiated via private ownership, single-source-code platform, native AI (vs Coupa's bolt-on Navi AI), extensive R&D investment, and multi-tenant architecture with seamless upgrades (vs Coupa's costly change-order model). Roeland did not defend Coupa or express satisfaction; instead he critiqued their existing CLM tool (which may or may not be Coupa's CLM module, but is clearly inadequate for strategic sourcing). The competitive wedge is Zycus's unified S2C platform with native AI and upgrade-path simplicity vs Coupa's fragmented, change-order-heavy, marketing-led approach. Threat level is medium-to-high: Coupa is the incumbent (switching cost, inertia, existing user base), but Roeland's dissatisfaction and proactive exploration of Zycus signal openness to displacement. No other competitors named; 'do nothing' (continue with inadequate CLM + Coupa status quo) is the secondary risk if the business case fails to justify investment and CIO roadmap slot.

sources: Avoma 22 Apr, Roeland: 'we do not see a value from the strategic sourcing point of view from that CLM tool... we would love to have a one platform having all of all of the capabilities'
Avoma note: 'Aspen has a CLM tool external to strategic sourcing but finds it lacking from the strategic sourcing perspective, motivating interest in Zycus's integrated platform'
Pain owner: Roeland Mvokwe (Group Digital Tech Strategic Sourcing Manager) and the strategic sourcing team; Kelly-Ann Schooling (Head of Procurement) likely at leadership level
identify_pain.narrative: Aspen's pain is tool fragmentation and lack of strategic sourcing capability in their current CLM system. Roeland stated on 22 Apr: 'we already have a CLM discussion external to strategic sourcing, and that's because the legal team was looking at a tool that would service them, then as a strategic sourcing team, we thought of including our requirements... and so far, we do not see a value from the strategic sourcing point of view from that CLM tool.' The legal-led CLM tool fails to serve the sourcing team's use case (sourcing events, supplier collaboration, contract lifecycle from a sourcing lens), forcing Aspen to operate with disparate, incomplete tools. The pain owner is Roeland and the strategic sourcing function (his team); Kelly-Ann Schooling (Head of Procurement and Supply Chain) presumably feels this pain at the leadership level but was not on the call. The pain is qualitative (workflow inefficiency, lack of unified platform) rather than quantified (no stated cost of the gap, manual hours wasted, or cycle-time impact). Roeland's urgent tone ('we would love to have a one platform') and his proactive outreach to Zycus at an awards dinner signal real, felt pain, but the business case he requested will be required to quantify the cost of inaction for CIO sign-off.

sources: Avoma 22 Apr: no contracting, legal, InfoSec, or procurement-channel discussion
Salesforce Current_Contract_Expiration__c: not populated
Roeland's stated sequence: business case → CIO roadmap approval → RFP (paper process comes post-RFP)
paper_process.narrative: Paper process (contracting, legal, InfoSec, procurement mechanics) is unknown at this early Qualified stage. Roeland's focus on 22 Apr was business case, pricing model, and roadmap approval; no discussion of MSA/SOW, legal review, InfoSec questionnaire, or procurement channel. Given Aspen's pharma sector (regulated, likely stringent security/compliance requirements) and enterprise scale, a formal InfoSec review and legal redline cycle are probable but unaddressed. Current_Contract_Expiration__c is not populated in Salesforce (no forcing function). No SI/partner involvement mentioned. Paper process visibility will come only after the CIO approves roadmap inclusion and Roeland launches the RFP (his stated sequence). Recommended move: during business-case delivery, surface InfoSec/legal requirements proactively so Zycus can pre-position security documentation and contracting templates for the RFP phase.

sources: SF Contact Role: Kelly-Ann Schooling, Decision Maker (no engagement evidence)
Avoma 22 Apr: Roeland 'I need our CIO to take this requirement... into a roadmap' (CIO = budget gatekeeper)
Avoma 22 Apr: Roeland 'bring Zycus to speak to both myself, manager, as well as the CIO' (EB = CIO + Roeland's manager)
No commercial/pricing conversation on the call; only a request for a future business case to present upward
economic_buyer.narrative: Economic Buyer is unmapped. Kelly-Ann Schooling (Head of Procurement and Supply Chain Management) holds the Decision Maker contact role, signaling final sign-off authority, but no evidence she controls budget or engaged in commercial/pricing discussion. The single discovery call (22 Apr) was led by Roeland Mvokwe (Group Digital Tech Strategic Sourcing Manager, Evaluator role), who requested a business case 'to bring Zycus to speak to both myself, manager, as well as the CIO so that we can have this capability' — naming his manager and the CIO as the budget/roadmap gatekeepers, not himself. No EB-titled contact (CFO, CPO, CIO) appears in contact roles or on the call. Roeland's statement reveals budget authority sits above him ('I do not have this not yet with the root in in our technology roadmap, and I need our CIO to to take this requirement'), and he needs the business case to sell internally. The CIO is the likely Economic Buyer (technology roadmap, budget allocation), but is unidentified and unengaged. Recommended move: map and engage the CIO and Roeland's manager (procurement head) with the tailored business case Roeland requested.

sources: Avoma 22 Apr, Roeland: 'bring Zycus to speak to both myself, manager, as well as the CIO... I need our CIO to take this requirement into a roadmap... so I can move forward with business case and be able to go into an RFP later on'
SF Contact Role: Kelly-Ann Schooling (Decision Maker), no engagement yet
Call sequence: single discovery call 22 Apr, no subsequent stakeholder escalation or follow-up
decision_process.narrative: The decision process is emerging but incomplete. Roeland Mvokwe (sourcing manager, Evaluator) is the active champion driving internal evaluation; he explicitly laid out the approval path on 22 Apr: 'I want you to keep that and maybe in the background work on a business case for us... to bring Zycus to speak to both myself, manager, as well as the CIO so that we can have this capability. Because I do not have this not yet... in our technology roadmap, and I need our CIO to take this requirement... into a roadmap, route map, planning. So I can move forward with business case and be able to go into an RFP later on.' This reveals a three-stage internal process: (1) Roeland receives business case and pricing from Zycus, (2) Roeland + his manager + the CIO review and approve adding S2C to the IT roadmap, (3) formal RFP launched once roadmap slot secured. Kelly-Ann Schooling (Decision Maker contact role) will presumably sign off at the end, but her role in the interim approval chain is unclear. The call sequence itself was minimal (one discovery call, no follow-up), so additional stakeholder engagement (manager, CIO, Kelly-Ann) has not yet occurred. Roeland's ask for a 'high level in the next few months' suggests a multi-month internal review before RFP, placing this deal in a long pre-formal-evaluation hold.

sources: Avoma 22 Apr, Roeland: unified S2C platform need, SAP integration requirement, pharma references, implementation speed, support quality, cost/business case
Avoma note: 'Aspen requires... strategic sourcing and contract lifecycle management to replace disparate tools'
Avoma note: 'Aspen wants a platform that avoids costly change orders and supports seamless upgrades'
Avoma note: 'Aspen signals the need for quick implementation turnaround and reliable ongoing support with direct contact options beyond ticketing systems'
decision_criteria.narrative: Aspen's evaluation criteria are explicit from the discovery call (22 Apr). Roeland stated the need for a unified strategic sourcing and contract lifecycle management platform ('one platform having all of all of the capabilities that we wanted from the street sourcing'), with integration to their SAP finance system ('our finances currently running on SAP... How is your experience of Zycus integrating to SAP'), fast implementation turnaround ('what does your implementation look like in terms of turnaround'), reliable post-go-live support ('what are your typical support timelines post implementation'), and pharmaceutical industry proof points ('what do you guys have the pharmaceutical industry customers that you have recently worked with... in the global space'). Roeland also emphasized cost-effectiveness via a business case before proceeding ('give us a business case for Aspen... what the cost model or the pricing model... look at an example of just one user here'). Additionally, Aspen requires seamless upgrade paths without customization jeopardy ('Aspen wants a platform that avoids costly change orders and supports seamless upgrades to the latest version without jeopardizing customizations', Avoma note), and easy user adoption with minimal change management (interest in Merlin AI-driven Teams intake). Criteria are clear; Zycus has not yet formally validated fit or submitted the requested business case.

ai_fit_signal.summary: Aspen is AI Curious. Roeland showed genuine interest in Zycus's AI capabilities on the discovery call (Merlin guided intake, autonomous negotiation agents, supplier onboarding automation) and engaged actively with the AI demonstration. Caroline noted 'Aspen showed interest in Zycus's AI-driven guided intake via Merlin bot, AI personal assistant for procurement tasks, and autonomous negotiation agents' and 'Zycus's AI agents can autonomously reorder non-strategic items, negotiate prices with suppliers, and onboard suppliers with risk checks, impressing Aspen.' However, Roeland's primary asks were business case, pricing, and pharma proof points (traditional evaluation criteria), not AI-led outcomes. No quantified AI value case was discussed (no stated goal for cycle-time reduction, automation rate, or AI-driven savings). AI is a differentiator that impressed Roeland, but the deal is not AI-led (it is pain-led, with unified S2C platform as the core need and AI as an enabling feature). To elevate to AI Hungry, the business case must quantify AI-driven ROI and make Merlin the hero of the CIO presentation. No AIS Score in Salesforce to corroborate.

Zycus committed to deliver business case, pharma client list, and value-case details; Roeland requested these 'in the next few weeks or months' to present to his manager and CIO for IT roadmap approval before launching RFP. No follow-up engagement since: LastActivityDate 08 May 2026 (49 days ago), no logged Task completion, no buyer reply. Deal has cooled; re-engagement required.

items.detail: Economic Buyer (CIO) and Roeland's manager unmapped and unengaged; deal is single-threaded through Roeland (Evaluator) who cannot approve budget or IT roadmap slot alone. Without CIO engagement, the S2C initiative will not be funded or launched, and 'do nothing' inertia will prevail.

Close date 22 Apr 2027 (300 days out) is a placeholder with no validated buyer timeline. Roeland stated he needs the business case 'in the next few weeks or months' to present to CIO for roadmap approval, then launch RFP ('so I can move forward with business case and be able to go into an RFP later on'). No forcing function (no contract expiration, no fiscal deadline, no compelling event stated). Timeline is buyer-dependent and unanchored; without near-term milestones the deal will drift.

Pricing and cost model are unvalidated. Roeland explicitly requested 'what the cost model or the pricing model of Zycus uses... look at an example of just one user here' but Zycus has not delivered. Without a credible pricing envelope anchored to Aspen's scale (products in scope: iContract, iSource, ML iAnalyze, iSave, iSupplier, Merlin Intake), the business case cannot be completed and CIO approval is blocked.

Aspen emphasized need for easy user adoption and minimal change management (interest in Merlin AI Teams intake, critique of current CLM tool complexity). While Zycus positioned no-training, seamless-adoption AI intake on the call, the change-management plan for Aspen's user base (scale, geographies, current Coupa familiarity) has not been scoped or validated. Implementation speed expectation (6-8 months) was stated but not formally agreed.

49-day engagement gap post-discovery with no buyer reply to promised deliverables. Roeland requested business case, pharma references, and presentation deck on 22 Apr; Zycus committed but no follow-up logged. Deal is cooling; without immediate re-engagement the opportunity risks being deprioritized or displaced by internal competing priorities.

champion_strength.summary: Champion is developing but at risk of weakening. Roeland Mvokwe proactively initiated contact, articulated Aspen's pain, and requested business case and pharma proof points to present internally to his manager and CIO. However, he has not yet demonstrated access to or influence over leadership (no introductions, no joint calls), and there has been no follow-up engagement for 65 days since the discovery call (no reply to promised deliverables, no Tasks logged). Champion is cooling; without immediate re-engagement and delivery of the requested business case, the opportunity risks stalling or being deprioritized internally.

items.action: Deliver the tailored business case with the relevant stakeholder model and pharma proof points to Roeland within 7 days.

Without this, CIO roadmap approval is blocked and the deal cannot advance to RFP.
items.expected_effect: Restart engagement, unblock CIO presentation, enable Roeland to secure IT roadmap slot and launch RFP. Business case must quantify ROI using Aspen's pain (CLM inadequacy, tool fragmentation), Zycus pharma proof points (Regeneron 70% cycle-time improvement, 200% onboarding speed), and pricing envelope for products in scope (iContract, iSource, ML iAnalyze, iSave, iSupplier, Merlin Intake). Include Merlin AI and autonomous agents as the wedge vs Coupa's bolt-on Navi AI. Position as co-authored with Roeland to ensure it addresses CIO priorities.

items.action: Request joint business-case review session with Roeland, his manager, and the CIO to present ROI case and validate IT roadmap fit.
items.trigger: Roeland named CIO and his manager as required approvers ('bring Zycus to speak to both myself, manager, as well as the CIO... I need our CIO to take this requirement into a roadmap'), but neither has engaged. Single-threaded through Roeland (Evaluator) in a deal requiring CIO budget/roadmap approval is high-risk. Business case delivery is the natural entry point to escalate.
items.expected_effect: Map and engage Economic Buyer (CIO) and Roeland's manager, validate S2C roadmap priority and budget alignment, multi-thread executive sponsorship. Position as 'ensuring the business case addresses CIO priorities' rather than a cold ask. Deliver Merlin AI and SAP HANA out-of-the-box integration as CIO-level value propositions. Secure commitment to next milestone (formal eval, RFP scope, or timeline confirmation).

items.action: Send pharma client references and the discovery call presentation deck to Roeland immediately.
items.trigger: Roeland explicitly requested pharma references ('what do you guys have the pharmaceutical industry customers... in the global space') and presentation deck ('share this presentation with me') on 22 Apr; Zycus committed but has not delivered for 65 days. These are table-stakes deliverables blocking Roeland's internal advocacy.
items.expected_effect: Demonstrate responsiveness, unblock Roeland's internal presentation to his manager/CIO, rebuild trust after 65-day delay. Include Regeneron pharma case study (70% cycle-time improvement, 200% onboarding speed, 20% supplier performance gain) plus 2-3 other pharma references with contact details for Roeland to validate directly.

items.action: Map Kelly-Ann Schooling (Decision Maker, Head of Procurement and Supply Chain) role in the approval chain and request introductory call.

Her role in the approval chain (final contract sign-off, budget co-approver, or advisory) is unclear; must be validated to ensure alignment with Roeland's S2C initiative and CIO roadmap approval.
items.expected_effect: Multi-thread to procurement leadership, validate Decision Maker sign-off authority, ensure Kelly-Ann is aligned with Roeland's initiative and not a blocker. Position as 'understanding procurement priorities for the S2C business case' to gain her input and advocacy.

items.action: Position Merlin AI and SAP HANA integration as wedge vs Coupa in the CIO business case presentation.
items.trigger: Coupa is the incumbent (medium threat due to switching cost) but Roeland critiqued their CLM tool inadequacy ('we do not see a value from the strategic sourcing point of view from that CLM tool'). Zycus positioned native AI (Merlin, autonomous agents) and SAP HANA out-of-the-box integration on 22 Apr, but the wedge is not yet formalized in writing or exec-level messaging. CIO presentation is the activation moment.
items.expected_effect: Make Coupa displacement inevitable by anchoring Zycus's ROI advantage to Aspen's pain (CLM inadequacy, tool fragmentation) and quantifying AI-driven efficiency gains (Merlin intake cycle-time reduction, autonomous agent supplier onboarding speed). Contrast Zycus native AI (32 patents, built-in) vs Coupa bolt-on Navi AI, and Zycus SAP HANA adapter (out-of-the-box, 80% of Zycus clients use SAP) vs Coupa custom integration overhead. Position Merlin as the hero of the CIO roadmap case.

Time in Qualified stage: 65 days with no progression. No buyer-facing deliverable executed in 60+ days forces Off Track verdict.
evidence: Single discovery call on 22 Apr 2026 (65 days ago) with Roeland Mvokwe requesting pharma references, business case, and pricing.

Roeland requested pricing quote and business use cases on that call; Next_Step__c reflects this: 'Pricing quote to be shared, while sending across some business use case' (no completion date recorded).
No subsequent buyer engagement: LastActivityDate 08 May 2026 (49 days ago), no follow-up calls, no logged Task completion of promised deliverables.

Stage Qualified with 300-day runway suggests placeholder close date; typical enterprise motion requires discovery depth, demos, RFP/shortlist, use-case validation before Vendor Selected (12-15 months out is realistic for early-stage, but engagement must be continuous).
Decision Maker Kelly-Ann Schooling identified in contact roles but no evidence of engagement or EB-level conversation.
Cooling engagement confirmed by server pulse: rep-side activity 49 days ago, no buyer reply.
north_star_verdict.headline: Deal remains dead 49 days since any activity and 65 days since sole discovery call. No buyer re-engagement, no deliverables delivered, no champion activation, no progression from Qualified. Early-stage deal stalled without path forward.

best_practice_check.summary: Early-stage qualified deal with strong initial buyer interest but stalled for 49 days post-discovery. Three critical levers to restart momentum and win: (1) deliver the requested business case with Aspen-specific ROI using pharma proof points to unlock CIO roadmap approval and RFP launch; (2) map and engage the Economic Buyer (CIO) and Roeland's manager (procurement head) to convert Roeland's internal advocacy into multi-threaded executive sponsorship; (3) position Merlin AI and autonomous agents as the wedge vs Coupa's bolt-on AI to make switching inevitable. Single biggest blocker: without the business case Roeland requested 65 days ago, the deal cannot advance (CIO will not add S2C to IT roadmap, no RFP, no progression). Risk of 'do nothing' inertia is high if Zycus does not re-engage with deliverables and executive alignment this week.

competitive_position.summary: Coupa is the sole active competitor and incumbent, holding medium-to-high threat due to switching cost and installed base, but Roeland's dissatisfaction with their current CLM tool (possibly Coupa CLM) and his proactive exploration of Zycus signal displacement opportunity. Zycus's wedge is native AI (Merlin intake, autonomous agents), unified S2C platform, private-ownership R&D focus, and seamless multi-tenant upgrades without change orders, vs Coupa's bolt-on Navi AI, fragmented acquisitions, and costly customization model. The primary current threat is 'do nothing' inertia: if the business case fails to justify CIO roadmap inclusion and investment, Aspen will continue with inadequate status quo (Coupa + separate CLM tool) rather than switch. No other vendors named; no shortlist yet (deal is pre-RFP, pending CIO approval).

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.quote: Aspen currently uses Coupa and is exploring Zycus as an alternative, noting Coupa's marketing focus versus Zycus's tech development emphasis. Roeland critiqued existing CLM tool: 'we do not see a value from the strategic sourcing point of view from that CLM tool.'
competitors.source: Avoma discovery call 22 Apr 2026, Roeland Mvokwe; Salesforce Competitors__c
competitors.how_we_win: Position Merlin AI-driven intake (native, autonomous agents) and unified S2C platform with seamless SAP integration and upgrade simplicity vs Coupa's bolt-on Navi AI, fragmented modules, and change-order dependency. Anchor the business case to Aspen's stated pain (CLM inadequacy) and deliver pharma proof points (Regeneron 70% cycle-time improvement) to justify CIO roadmap slot and RFP launch.

competitors.name: Do Nothing / Status Quo
competitors.quote: Roeland stated 'I do not have this not yet... in our technology roadmap, and I need our CIO to take this requirement... into a roadmap' — revealing that without CIO approval and a compelling business case, the S2C initiative will remain unfunded and Aspen will continue with current inadequate tools.
competitors.source: Avoma 22 Apr 2026, Roeland; inferred from CIO roadmap-approval requirement
competitors.how_we_win: Deliver the requested business case with quantified ROI (Aspen's own metrics: cycle time, supplier onboarding speed, cost savings) and pharma proof points to make the cost of inaction (continuing with fragmented, inefficient tools) unacceptable. Make the CIO roadmap approval inevitable by co-authoring the internal business case with Roeland.

I want to have a high level in the next few months to bring Zycus to speak to both myself, manager, as well as the CIO.'

ai_positioning_strength.score: Medium (strong initial interest, not yet formalized or executive-activated)
ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI positioning is strong on the discovery call but not yet activated in writing or at the executive level. Roeland showed genuine interest in Merlin AI-driven intake (Microsoft Teams embedded, minimal change management), autonomous negotiation agents, and supplier onboarding automation. Caroline positioned Zycus's native AI (32 patents, built-in vs Coupa's bolt-on Navi AI) as a key differentiator. However, the AI value proposition has not been formalized in the requested business case, quantified in ROI terms, or presented to the CIO (the roadmap gatekeeper). To convert interest into commitment, the business case must anchor Merlin and autonomous agents to Aspen's pain (CLM inadequacy, tool fragmentation, manual inefficiency) and quantify the efficiency gain (e.g. Regeneron 70% cycle-time improvement, 200% onboarding speed). AI is the wedge vs Coupa; it must become the centerpiece of the CIO presentation.

items.quote: Roeland: 'we would love to have a one platform having all of all of the capabilities that we wanted from the street sourcing.' Zycus presented full S2C suite including iSource (sourcing) and iContract (contract management).
items.criterion: Unified strategic sourcing and contract lifecycle management platform

items.quote: Roeland: 'our finances currently running on SAP. How is your experience of Zycus integrating to SAP?' Caroline: 'since we only work with enterprise clients, 80% of our clients are on SAP... We have an out of the box connector to SAP for HANA.'

Roeland showed interest in Merlin AI-driven guided intake via Microsoft Teams. Avoma note: 'Aspen showed interest in Zycus's AI-driven guided intake via Merlin bot, AI personal assistant for procurement tasks, and autonomous negotiation agents.' Caroline: 'you don't need any training or change management... it works inside Microsoft Teams or Slack. So it's no friction, seamless adoption.'
items.source: Avoma 22 Apr discovery call; Avoma AI excitement note
items.criterion: Easy user adoption with minimal change management (AI-driven intake)

items.quote: Roeland: 'what do you guys have the pharmaceutical industry customers that you have recently worked with... in the global space?' Caroline cited Regeneron pharma client with '70% improvement in cycle time, 200% supplier onboarding speed, 20% supplier performance score' and committed to send more pharma references and business cases. Delivery pending.
items.source: Avoma 22 Apr discovery call; Avoma action-item note
items.criterion: Pharmaceutical industry proof points and references

items.quote: Roeland: 'what does your implementation look like in terms of turnaround... and what are your typical support timelines post implementation?' Caroline: 'an implementation takes 6 to 8 months... We have premium support and regular support... hypercare after our clients go live... you can actually call us... we have also a Whatsapp number... a customer value manager that is dedicated to you.'
items.criterion: Fast implementation turnaround and reliable post-go-live support

items.quote: Avoma note: 'Aspen wants a platform that avoids costly change orders and supports seamless upgrades to the latest version without jeopardizing customizations.' Caroline: 'we are a multi tenant platform... everyone is always automatically on the next release... all the different things that only you need, we're doing an app extend outside. So you will never have to have any change order if you wanna go in the next release.'
items.source: Avoma 22 Apr discovery call; Avoma implicit-requirements note
items.criterion: Seamless upgrades without customization jeopardy or costly change orders

items.quote: Roeland: 'give us a business case for Aspen... what the cost model or the pricing model of Zycus... look at an example of just one user here... I want you to keep that and maybe in the background work on a business case for us.' Zycus committed but has not yet delivered; cost-effectiveness remains unvalidated.
items.source: Avoma 22 Apr discovery call; Avoma action-item note
items.criterion: Cost-effectiveness and ROI (business case with pricing model)

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Aspen's expectations align well with Zycus's platform capabilities across unified S2C, SAP integration, AI-driven intake, supplier collaboration, and seamless upgrades. Key fit areas confirmed on discovery call: Merlin AI intake in Microsoft Teams (strong buyer interest in easy adoption, minimal change management), SAP HANA out-of-the-box integration (Aspen runs SAP finance, Zycus has HANA adapter), modular platform with data migration and controls setup (Roeland asked about migration and preferred-vendor layering, Zycus confirmed full implementation support), and pharma industry proof points (Zycus cited Regeneron pharma client with strong results). Gaps: Zycus has not yet delivered the requested business case, pharma client references, or pricing model, so fit remains unvalidated from Aspen's internal decision-making perspective. Implementation speed expectation (Roeland asked about 'turnaround') was met (Zycus cited 6-8 months), but no formal timeline discussed. Cost-effectiveness and ROI are unproven pending business case delivery.

hard.ais_why:
• Apr 2026 Avoma call captures Caroline Lacocque pitching the 'AI-forward platform' with autonomous agents and native AI to Aspen Holdings. Aspen's buyer Roeland engaged actively on the call but his questions centred on operational topics ‚Äî data migration, support model, SAP integration, pharma reference customers ‚Äî and did not lead with AI-specific asks. AI was Zycus-positioned; buyer engagement was operational.
• Modest AI products in scope (Merlin Intake and ML iAnalyze) alongside traditional S2C modules (iContract, iSave, iSource, iSupplier). BD_Engagement is Engaged. Recent LastActivityDate 2026-05-08. A business case has been requested by Aspen ‚Äî the deal is moving forward but the buyer's stated focus is operational fit not AI capability.
• Medium confidence: clear call evidence of mixed signals ‚Äî Zycus pitched AI but Aspen engaged on operational fundamentals. The customer is engaged but not AI-driven. Defaulted to 5 at the 5/6 boundary as AI is not the explicit selection criterion. Would move UP to 7 if Roeland or a colleague returns with AI-specific follow-up questions; would move DOWN to 4 if the SAP integration / pharma references dominate evaluation.
hard.next_step: Pricing quote to be shared , while sending across some business use case .

deltas.subject: Economic Buyer (CIO) and Roeland's manager unmapped and unengaged. Roeland named the CIO as the IT roadmap and budget gatekeeper ('I need our CIO to take this requirement into a roadmap') and his manager as a required co-approver ('bring Zycus to speak to both myself, manager, as well as the CIO'), but neither has engaged with the relevant stakeholder through Roeland (Evaluator role) in a deal requiring CIO roadmap approval is high-risk; Roeland can champion but cannot fund or approve alone. Recommended move: use the business-case delivery meeting to request a joint session with Roeland, his manager, and the CIO to present the ROI case and validate roadmap fit (position as 'ensuring the business case addresses CIO priorities' rather than a cold ask). Map Kelly-Ann Schooling (Decision Maker contact role, Head of Procurement and Supply Chain) as well to understand her sign-off role.

deltas.to: Coupa displacement strategy incomplete. Coupa is the incumbent (medium threat due to switching cost and installed base) but Roeland's dissatisfaction with their
deltas.subject: Coupa displacement strategy incomplete. Coupa is the incumbent (medium threat due to switching cost and installed base) but Roeland's dissatisfaction with their CLM tool and his proactive Zycus exploration signal displacement opportunity. Zycus positioned native AI (Merlin, autonomous agents) and seamless upgrades vs Coupa's bolt-on Navi AI and change-order dependency on the discovery call, but the wedge is not yet activated in writing (no business case anchoring it, no proof-point comparison, no exec-level messaging). The business case must make Coupa's limitations explicit (bolt-on AI, fragmented modules, customization rigidity, CLM inadequacy for strategic sourcing) and quantify Zycus's ROI advantage using Aspen's pain and pharma proof points. Also surface early in CIO conversation: SAP HANA out-of-the-box integration (Zycus strength, likely Coupa gap) and multi-tenant seamless-upgrade model (Zycus differentiator, counters Coupa change-order fatigue Roeland may have experienced).

Likely procurement or sourcing leadership; may be Shanne Maistry (Head Group Digital Technology Strategic Sourcing) or Kelly-Ann Schooling, but unclear. Must be identified and engaged to validate Roeland's internal advocacy and secure approval path.
value.title: Unknown (likely Head of Procurement or Sourcing)
value.source: Avoma 22 Apr, Roeland: 'bring Zycus to speak to both myself, manager'; identity unclear

packets.subject: Roeland's manager (unidentified)

value.name: Coupa
value.quote: Aspen currently uses Coupa and is exploring Zycus as an alternative, noting Coupa's marketing focus versus Zycus's tech development emphasis. Roeland critiqued existing CLM tool: 'we do not see a value from the strategic sourcing point of view from that CLM tool.'
value.source: Avoma discovery call 22 Apr 2026, Roeland Mvokwe; Salesforce Competitors__c
value.how_we_win: Position Merlin AI-driven intake (native, autonomous agents) and unified S2C platform with seamless SAP integration and upgrade simplicity vs Coupa's bolt-on Navi AI, fragmented modules, and change-order dependency. Anchor the business case to Aspen's stated pain (CLM inadequacy) and deliver pharma proof points (Regeneron 70% cycle-time improvement) to justify CIO roadmap slot and RFP launch.

packets.subject: Coupa

value.name: Do Nothing / Status Quo
value.quote: Roeland stated 'I do not have this not yet... in our technology roadmap, and I need our CIO to take this requirement... into a roadmap' — revealing that without CIO approval and a compelling business case, the S2C initiative will remain unfunded and Aspen will continue with current inadequate tools.
value.source: Avoma 22 Apr 2026, Roeland; inferred from CIO roadmap-approval requirement
value.how_we_win: Deliver the requested business case with quantified ROI (Aspen's own metrics: cycle time, supplier onboarding speed, cost savings) and pharma proof points to make the cost of inaction (continuing with fragmented, inefficient tools) unacceptable. Make the CIO roadmap approval inevitable by co-authoring the internal business case with Roeland.

packets.subject: Do Nothing / Status Quo

value.detail: Economic Buyer (CIO) and Roeland's manager unmapped and unengaged; deal is single-threaded through Roeland (Evaluator) who cannot approve budget or IT roadmap slot alone. Without CIO engagement, the S2C initiative will not be funded or launched, and 'do nothing' inertia will prevail.
value.source: Avoma 22 Apr, Roeland: 'I need our CIO to take this requirement into a roadmap'; no CIO or manager engagement

packets.subject: Economic Buyer (CIO) and Roeland's manager unmapped and unengaged; deal is single-threaded through Roeland (Evaluator) who cannot approve budget or IT roadmap slot alone. Without CIO engagement, the S2C initiative will not be funded or launched, and 'do nothing' inertia will prevail.

value.detail: Close date 22 Apr 2027 (300 days out) is a placeholder with no validated buyer timeline. Roeland stated he needs the business case 'in the next few weeks or months' to present to CIO for roadmap approval, then launch RFP ('so I can move forward with business case and be able to go into an RFP later on'). No forcing function (no contract expiration, no fiscal deadline, no compelling event stated). Timeline is buyer-dependent and unanchored; without near-term milestones the deal will drift.
value.source: Avoma 22 Apr, Roeland; Salesforce CloseDate 2027-04-22 (no validated timeline)

packets.subject: Close date 22 Apr 2027 (300 days out) is a placeholder with no validated buyer timeline. Roeland stated he needs the business case 'in the next few weeks or months' to present to CIO for roadmap approval, then launch RFP ('so I can move forward with business case and be able to go into an RFP later on'). No forcing function (no contract expiration, no fiscal deadline, no compelling event stated). Timeline is buyer-dependent and unanchored; without near-term milestones the deal will drift.

value.detail: Pricing and cost model are unvalidated. Roeland explicitly requested 'what the cost model or the pricing model of Zycus uses... look at an example of just one user here' but Zycus has not delivered. Without a credible pricing envelope anchored to Aspen's scale (products in scope: iContract, iSource, ML iAnalyze, iSave, iSupplier, Merlin Intake), the business case cannot be completed and CIO approval is blocked.
value.source: Avoma 22 Apr, Roeland request; no pricing delivered

packets.subject: Pricing and cost model are unvalidated. Roeland explicitly requested 'what the cost model or the pricing model of Zycus uses... look at an example of just one user here' but Zycus has not delivered. Without a credible pricing envelope anchored to Aspen's scale (products in scope: iContract, iSource, ML iAnalyze, iSave, iSupplier, Merlin Intake), the business case cannot be completed and CIO approval is blocked.

value.detail: Aspen emphasized need for easy user adoption and minimal change management (interest in Merlin AI Teams intake, critique of current CLM tool complexity). While Zycus positioned no-training, seamless-adoption AI intake on the call, the change-management plan for Aspen's user base (scale, geographies, current Coupa familiarity) has not been scoped or validated. Implementation speed expectation (6-8 months) was stated but not formally agreed.
value.source: Avoma 22 Apr; Roeland asked about implementation turnaround and support; Caroline cited 6-8 months

value.detail: 49-day engagement gap post-discovery with no buyer reply to promised deliverables. Roeland requested business case, pharma references, and presentation deck on 22 Apr; Zycus committed but no follow-up logged. Deal is cooling; without immediate re-engagement the opportunity risks being deprioritized or displaced by internal competing priorities.
value.source: LastActivityDate 08 May 2026 (49 days ago); no follow-up engagement

value.flag: Business case and pharma references overdue 65 days. Roeland explicitly requested tailored business case with pricing model and pharma client proof points on 22 Apr ('give us a business case for Aspen... in the next few weeks or months... to bring Zycus to speak to... the CIO'); Zycus committed but has not delivered. Without this, Roeland cannot present to his manager or CIO, the IT roadmap slot will not be secured, and the deal cannot progress to the relevant stakeholder is the single highest-priority action: co-author the business case with Roeland using Aspen's pain points (CLM inadequacy, tool fragmentation), Zycus pharma proof points (Regeneron 70% cycle-time improvement), and an ROI model anchored to Aspen's scale. Deliver within 7 days to restart momentum.

Deliver within 7 days to restart momentum.

value.flag: Economic Buyer (CIO) and Roeland's manager unmapped and unengaged. Roeland named the CIO as the IT roadmap and budget gatekeeper ('I need our CIO to take this requirement into a roadmap') and his manager as a required co-approver ('bring Zycus to speak to both myself, manager, as well as the CIO'), but neither has engaged with the relevant stakeholder through Roeland (Evaluator role) in a deal requiring CIO roadmap approval is high-risk; Roeland can champion but cannot fund or approve alone. Recommended move: use the business-case delivery meeting to request a joint session with Roeland, his manager, and the CIO to present the ROI case and validate roadmap fit (position as 'ensuring the business case addresses CIO priorities' rather than a cold ask). Map Kelly-Ann Schooling (Decision Maker contact role, Head of Procurement and Supply Chain) as well to understand her sign-off role.

packets.subject: Economic Buyer (CIO) and Roeland's manager unmapped and unengaged. Roeland named the CIO as the IT roadmap and budget gatekeeper ('I need our CIO to take this requirement into a roadmap') and his manager as a required co-approver ('bring Zycus to speak to both myself, manager, as well as the CIO'), but neither has engaged with the relevant stakeholder through Roeland (Evaluator role) in a deal requiring CIO roadmap approval is high-risk; Roeland can champion but cannot fund or approve alone. Recommended move: use the business-case delivery meeting to request a joint session with Roeland, his manager, and the CIO to present the ROI case and validate roadmap fit (position as 'ensuring the business case addresses CIO priorities' rather than a cold ask). Map Kelly-Ann Schooling (Decision Maker contact role, Head of Procurement and Supply Chain) as well to understand her sign-off role.

value.flag: Coupa displacement strategy incomplete. Coupa is the incumbent (medium threat due to switching cost and installed base) but Roeland's dissatisfaction with their CLM tool and his proactive Zycus exploration signal displacement opportunity. Zycus positioned native AI (Merlin, autonomous agents) and seamless upgrades vs Coupa's bolt-on Navi AI and change-order dependency on the discovery call, but the wedge is not yet activated in writing (no business case anchoring it, no proof-point comparison, no exec-level messaging). The business case must make Coupa's limitations explicit (bolt-on AI, fragmented modules, customization rigidity, CLM inadequacy for strategic sourcing) and quantify Zycus's ROI advantage using Aspen's pain and pharma proof points. Also surface early in CIO conversation: SAP HANA out-of-the-box integration (Zycus strength, likely Coupa gap) and multi-tenant seamless-upgrade model (Zycus differentiator, counters Coupa change-order fatigue Roeland may have experienced).

scope

gaps: Only one buyer call (discovery 22 Apr 2026) limits depth of MEDDPICC coverage; no follow-up calls to validate progression, refine ROI case, or engage additional stakeholders.
Economic Buyer (CIO) and Roeland's manager are named as approval gatekeepers but are unidentified and unengaged; no contact roles, no call participation, no mapping.
Kelly-Ann Schooling (Decision Maker contact role) did not attend discovery call and has no engagement evidence; her role in approval chain and alignment with Roeland's initiative are unknown.
No quantified business value baseline from Aspen: no current-state cycle time, supplier onboarding speed, cost of CLM inadequacy, or manual-hours wasted captured on the call.
No formal timeline or forcing function validated; close date 22 Apr 2027 is a placeholder with no buyer-confirmed milestones or compelling event.
No discussion of paper process (legal, InfoSec, procurement mechanics) at this early stage; will surface post-RFP but is unaddressed.
AIS (AI excitement) score/status not populated in Salesforce; AI Curious tier inferred from call content only.
validation gate: a named person in a to-do/action item is in no Salesforce contact role, Avoma attendee list, or active-user roster and is anchored to no source (offending: 'Aspen ROI')
